description: Places a point at a specified angle from a baseline using the protractor instrument. The protractor auto-positions at the baseline midpoint. The result behaves as a point and can be used by line(), vector(), etc.
utility: Construct a point at a measured angle from a reference line — result is reusable as a point geometry

arguments.name: P1 or lineOrVector
arguments.description: First baseline endpoint (two-point form) or an existing line/vector as baseline

arguments.name: P2
arguments.description: Second baseline endpoint (only in two-point form)

arguments.name: angleDeg
arguments.description: The angle in degrees from the baseline to place the point

alternates.description: Define baseline with two points, place point at angle from midpoint
Use existing line or vector as baseline

examples.scenario: Place a point at 60° from a baseline defined by two points
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(-0.9, 6.6), 30, range(-3, 8), type(geometry)); P = protractorpoint(G, point(G, 2, 5), point(G, 4, 10), 60)

examples.scenario: Place two protractor points at 60° from different baselines
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(-0.9, 6.6), 30, range(-3, 8), type(geometry)); P = protractorpoint(G, point(G, 2, 5), point(G, 4, 10), 60); Q = protractorpoint(G, point(G, 4, 8), point(G, 4, 1), 60); line_1 = line(G, P, Q, type(line), c(steelblue), defer())

examples.scenario: Place a point at 60° from an existing line segment
examples.expression: G = g2d(at(2,11), size(21), range(-1, 6), type(geometry)); line_1 = line(G, point(G, 1.1, 3.8), point(G, 3.8, 1.7), type(segment), c(steelblue)); point_1 = protractorpoint(G, line_1, 60)
